headline: A Look At Phillips 66 (PSX) Valuation After Analyst Upgrades Dividend Hike And Venezuela Crude Moves
summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) has moved back onto investors’ radars after analyst upgrades, a higher quarterly dividend and a push to secure heavy crude from Venezuela, all set against a backdrop of rising global energy prices. See our latest analysis for Phillips 66. At a share price of US$159.78, Phillips 66 has paired a 1 month share price return of 11.3% with a 1 year total shareholder return of 35.45%. This suggests momentum has been building as dividend decisions, analyst commentary and its planned...

headline: U.S. crude oil retreats for the first session since Iran war began as Bessent pledges support
summary: The oil market has calmed after President Donald Trump said the U.S. would insure tankers  and provide naval escorts if necessary.

headline: Does Venezuela Heavy Crude Deal And Higher Dividend Change The Bull Case For Phillips 66 (PSX)?
summary: Phillips 66 recently saw its shares react to a mix of analyst upgrades, a higher quarterly dividend, and plans to source heavy crude directly from Venezuela starting April 2026, against a backdrop of tighter global energy markets. At the same time, CEO Mark Lashier’s upcoming appearance at the Morgan Stanley Energy & Power Conference offers investors fresh insight into how management is aligning capital allocation, refining feedstock choices, and long-term growth plans. We’ll now examine...

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Phillips 66 (PSX) After Its Strong 1 Year Run?
summary: If you are wondering whether Phillips 66 is still good value after its recent run, this article will walk through what the current share price could mean for you as an investor. The stock last closed at US$159.78, with returns of 3.5% over 7 days, 11.3% over 30 days, 22.4% year to date and 35.5% over 1 year. The 3 year and 5 year returns sit at 74.6% and 126.8% respectively. Recent attention on Phillips 66 has been shaped by ongoing coverage of the energy sector, including market commentary...

headline: Hideout in the Energy Sector? Here are the Top Oil Stocks to Watch
summary: The energy sector is the top-performing sector this year, boosted by a nearly 30% surge in crude prices amid disruptions in the Middle East.
